questions.question: What is the primary benefit of a shared CSS design system across pages?
questions.explanation: A shared design system ensures visual consistency and reduces code duplication across pages.

questions.question: How do you indicate the active page in navigation?
questions.explanation: aria-current='page' is the standard way to indicate the current page in navigation.

questions.question: What is the recommended CSS architecture for multi-page sites?
questions.explanation: A single shared stylesheet for the design system with optional page-specific files is best practice.

questions.question: What landmark role should be on every page's header?
questions.explanation: role='banner' identifies the site header as a landmark for screen readers.

questions.question: How do you create reusable card components?
questions.explanation: Reusable components use a base class with optional modifier classes for variations.

questions.question: What is the best way to manage CSS custom properties across pages?
options: Define all in :root in shared stylesheet
questions.explanation: All custom properties should be defined in :root in the shared stylesheet for global access.

questions.question: How do you create a consistent responsive grid system?
questions.explanation: A consistent grid system uses reusable classes like .grid-2, .grid-3 with media queries.

questions.question: What attribute ensures consistent skip-to-content across pages?
questions.explanation: Using the same id='main' on the <main> element ensures the skip link works consistently.

questions.question: What CSS approach ensures buttons look the same on every page?
questions.explanation: A .btn class with variations (.btn-primary, .btn-outline) in the shared stylesheet ensures consistency.

questions.question: How should you organize page-specific styles?
options: Page-specific CSS files or sections in main CSS
questions.explanation: Page-specific styles can be in separate files (about.css) or sections in the main stylesheet.

questions.question: What is the best approach for consistent form styling across pages?
options: Form component classes in the shared stylesheet
questions.explanation: Form component classes (.form-group, .form-input, .form-label) in the shared stylesheet ensure consistent form appearance.

questions.question: How do you create a responsive navigation that works across all pages?
options: Same nav HTML structure with shared CSS and a mobile toggle
questions.explanation: Consistent nav HTML structure with shared CSS and a responsive hamburger toggle ensures uniformity.

questions.question: What should a consistent footer include across multi-page sites?
options: Logo, nav links, social icons, copyright, all styled from shared CSS
questions.explanation: A consistent footer with logo, navigation, social links, and copyright styled from the shared stylesheet.

questions.question: How do you create a contact page with a form and info section?
options: Two-column grid with form on left and contact info on right, stacked on mobile
questions.explanation: A two-column grid layout with form and contact info that collapses to single column on mobile.

questions.question: What approach creates a consistent heading hierarchy across pages?
options: Typography classes (.h1-.h6) defined in the shared design system
questions.explanation: Typography utility or component classes in the shared stylesheet ensure consistent heading hierarchy.

questions.question: How do you implement a 404 page that matches the site design?
options: Centered layout with large error code, message, and link home button
questions.explanation: A centered layout with large 404 text, helpful message, and a button linking to the homepage.

questions.question: What CSS approach creates a consistent spacing system?
options: Utility classes like .mt-4, .mb-4 using CSS custom property spacing scale
questions.explanation: A spacing scale of CSS custom properties with utility classes ensures consistent rhythm across pages.

questions.question: How do you create a blog listing page that works with the design system?
options: Card grid using the shared card component classes
questions.explanation: Reusing the card component classes from the shared design system creates a consistent blog listing.

questions.question: What is the best approach for dark mode across a multi-page site?
options: CSS custom property toggle with data-theme on html element
questions.explanation: A data-theme attribute toggling CSS custom properties on the root element is the cleanest multi-page dark mode approach.

questions.question: What CSS media feature should be included for print styles across pages?
options: @media print in the shared stylesheet to hide nav/sidebar/ads
questions.explanation: A @media print query in the shared stylesheet that hides navigation, sidebars, and ads ensures print-friendly pages.
